title: Network fails on rootfull containers on Debian 10 (Buster)
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

With Debian 10 creating a rootfull container with a bridged network do not have access to the internet. Connections from host to the container works, but packets does not get forwarded and masqueraded to the hosts upstream interface.

Publishing ports fails explicitly with an error returned by iptables, but the setup of the bridged network seems to fail silently.

The default iptables implementation on Debian 10 is a wrapper around nftables. Changing to the iptables implementation to iptables-legacy (using the command 'update-alternatives --set iptables /usr/sbin/iptables-legacy') fixes the problem. I have not tried if using firewalld works as well.

Another untested alternative might be the nftables compatible CNI plugins at https://github.com/greenpau/cni-plugins

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.2.0
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.14
Built:        Thu Jan  1 01:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
"Publishing ports fails explicitly with an error returned by iptables, but the setup of the bridged network seems to fail silently." - Any chance you can provide that error?

Also, for further details: do you have any firewall management software (e.g. firewalld, ufw) installed? Can you provide the output of `iptables -nvL` and `iptables -t nat -nvL` both with and without a Podman container running?

We should be 100% compatible with nftables-backed iptables out of the box - RHEL 8 ships this configuration by default, and Podman is fully supported there - so I am leaning towards an environment issue.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we believe this is not a podman issue, I am closing.
